<<Data Conversion>>  

SECTION 1  
The JSON data provided contains three key-value pairs: "name", "age", and "city". These keys correspond to the individual's full name, age in years, and city of residence, respectively. The values are of different data types, including a string for the name and city, and an integer for the age.  

SECTION 2  
The corresponding Python dictionary can be constructed by directly mapping the JSON keys to their respective values. The resulting dictionary will have the structure:  
{"name": "John", "age": 30, "city": "New York"}  
This transformation preserves the original data while converting it into a Python dictionary format, ensuring the data remains neat.
